elements.sent-2411-001: Point cloud video (PCV) is a versatile 3D representation of dynamic scenes with many emerging applications.
elements.sent-2411-002: This paper introduces U-Motion, a learning-based compression scheme for both PCV geometry and attributes.
elements.sent-2411-003: We propose a U-Structured multiscale inter-frame prediction framework, U-Inter, which performs layer-wise explicit motion estimation and compensation (ME/MC) at different scales with varying levels of detail.
elements.sent-2411-004: It integrates both higher and lower-scale motion features, in addition to the information of current and previous frames, to enable accurate motion estimation at the current scale.
elements.sent-2411-005: In addition, we design a cascaded spatial predictive coding module to capture the inter-scale spatial redundancy remaining after U-Inter prediction.
elements.sent-2411-006: We further propose an effective {\it context detach and restore scheme to reduce spatial-temporal redundancy in the motion and latent bit-streams and improve compression performance.}
elements.sent-2411-007: We conduct experiments following the MPEG Common Test Condition and demonstrate that U-Motion can achieve significant gains over MPEG G-PCC-GesTM v3.0 and recently published learning-based methods for both geometry and attribute compression.
elements.sent-2411-008: Point cloud video is a versatile 3D representation with broad applications, including immersive volumetric video streaming, autonomous driving, and augmented reality~\cite{shan2024contrastive,hu2024low .}
elements.sent-2411-009: Due to the high data volume, it is essential to compress point cloud videos efficiently for data storage and transmission.
elements.sent-2411-010: This demand leads to the successful standardization of MPEG PCC (including G-PCC~\cite{gpccwhitepaper and V-PCC~\cite{graziosi2020overview ).}}
elements.sent-2411-011: Beyond these standards, numerous learning-based methods~\cite{wang2022sparse,sheng2021deep,zhang2023yoga have been proposed for compression of either static or dynamic point clouds, either for geometry (locations of points) or attributes (colors or other properties associated with points).}
elements.sent-2411-012: One essential element in point cloud video compression is inter-frame coding, which exploits the temporal redundancy between consecutive frames.
elements.sent-2411-013: This is typically accomplished through motion estimation and compensation.
elements.sent-2411-014: However, compared with 2D videos on a regular 2D grid, the points in a PCV are irregularly spread over a small fraction of the 3D space, making the design of motion estimation and compensation approaches for PCV significantly more challenging.
elements.sent-2411-015: Moreover, to ensure superior rate-distortion performance, we need to incorporate efficient context-based coding schemes to compress the estimated motion fields as well as features for recovering the geometry or attributes.
elements.sent-2411-016: Furthermore, it is desirable to develop a codec framework that can be used for both the geometry and attributes, to save hardware design resources for future chip tape-outs.. .
elements.sent-2411-017: To address the inter-coding problem, standard approaches either project the point cloud to a 2D video and use an existing video codec for motion estimation and compensation (i.e. V-PCC~\cite{graziosi2020overview ), or conduct cube-level motion compensation with a neighbor search strategy (i.e. G-PCC~\cite{gpccwhitepaper ).}}
elements.sent-2411-018: Meanwhile, some learned dynamic point cloud compression methods~\cite{wang2023dynamic,wang2024versatile1,wang2024versatile2 have been proposed to leverage sparse convolution techniques~\cite{choy20194d to exploit temporal correlation, known as {\it temporal convolution .}}}
elements.sent-2411-019: Specifically, the state-of-the-art scheme Unicorn~\cite{wang2024versatile1,wang2024versatile2 incorporates the spatial-temporal target convolution to predict the current frame from the reference frame.}
elements.sent-2411-020: However, these techniques limit the potential underlying motion patterns to be within the expressibility of a set of learned convolutional kernels, without the correct localization for temporal context mining offered by explicit motion estimation.
elements.sent-2411-021: This leads to suboptimal inter prediction.
elements.sent-2411-022: To tackle the challenge of motion estimation in point cloud videos, we propose a novel unified point cloud compression scheme, named U-Motion, which incorporates an U-Structured inter prediction module, named U-Inter, to estimate and compensate motion at different scales.
elements.sent-2411-023: The U-Inter structure, akin to the popular U-Net, through the use of both top-down, bottom-up and skip connections, leverages the motion information at both upper and lower scales to inform motion estimation at a current scale.
elements.sent-2411-024: We also propose novel network structures for {\it context detach and {\it context restore for compressing motion and frame features, using the coarser motion as the context for coding the current motion, and using both inter-context (motion-compensated from the reference frame) and intra-context (upsampled from lower scale) for coding the frame features.}}
elements.sent-2411-025: We evaluate our method following the MPEG Common Test Conditions (CTC)'s specification~\cite{m66563 for data splitting, in both geometry and color coding.}
elements.sent-2411-026: The experimental results show that U-Motion outperforms the learning-based state-of-the-art Unicorn~\cite{wang2024versatile2 , as well as the rule-based MPEG standard G-PCC-GesTM-v3.0~\cite{w23041 by a large margin in terms of rate-distortion (R-D) trade-off.}}
elements.sent-2411-027: We conduct ablation studies to validate the effectiveness of each component in our proposed U-Motion codec, and show that the proposed method is robust to different types of human point clouds and motion patterns.
elements.sent-2411-028: Our contributions can be summarized as follows:.
elements.sent-2411-029: We propose a novel unified PCV compression scheme, named U-Motion, which incorporates a hierarchical motion estimation module using a U-Net like structure.
elements.sent-2411-030: We demonstrate the importance of both top-down and bottom-up motion calculations as well as skip connections for accurate motion estimation and efficient motion coding under rate constraint.
elements.sent-2411-031: The framework can be used for both geometry and attribute coding.
elements.sent-2411-032: We compress the motion feature by contextually detaching the estimated motion at a lower layer, and conduct a 2-stage temporal-and-spatial {\it context detach to compress the attribute feature.}
elements.sent-2411-033: We evaluate our proposed U-Motion codec following the MPEG CTC's data splitting, for both geometry coding and color coding, and demonstrate U-Motion's significant performance gain against the learning-based state-of-the-art method Unicorn \cite{wang2024versatile2 and MPEG standard G-PCC-GesTM v3.0 \cite{w23041 in terms of rate-distortion trade-off.}}
elements.sent-2411-034: There are two primary technical routes for traditional point cloud video compression: 3D-structure-based and video-based.
elements.sent-2411-035: A notable example is the MPEG standard G-PCC-GesTM~\cite{w23041 , which has demonstrated state-of-the-art performance among all 3D-structure-based methods.}
elements.sent-2411-036: In contrast, 2D-video-based methods~\cite{zhu2020view,he2017best,graziosi2020overview reuse the existing 2D video codecs by projecting the 3D point cloud video into 2D space.}
elements.sent-2411-037: Among all 2D-video-based methods, the MPEG standard V-PCC achieves state-of-the-art performance.

elements.sent-2411-038: Early research on learning-based point cloud compression focuses on static point clouds~\cite{wang2021multiscale,wang2022sparse,fang20223dac,zhang2023yoga,fu2022octattention .}
elements.sent-2411-039: Wang et al.~\cite{wang2021multiscale first propose to use sparse convolution~\cite{choy20194d to compress voxelized point clouds, and propose a multiscale architecture, as well as a local auto-regressive architecture SOPA~\cite{wang2022sparse for lossless compression.}}}
elements.sent-2411-040: To further exploit the temporal redundancy in point cloud video, some methods adopt temporal convolution for inter prediction.
elements.sent-2411-041: Anique et al.~\cite{akhtar2024inter design a predictor for geometry compression, which compensates and compresses the current frame's latent representation based on its reference neighbors.}
elements.sent-2411-042: Wang et al. introduce Unicorn~\cite{wang2024versatile1,wang2024versatile2 , a unified architecture that integrates geometry \& attributes, static \& dynamic compression, using context compensation and multiscale motion compensation.}
elements.sent-2411-043: The temporal compensation is obtained through sparse convolution on the voxels in the previous frame, known as target convolution.
elements.sent-2411-044: However, both Unicorn and Anique et al.'s method lack explicit motion estimation, leading to sub-optimal temporal prediction.
elements.sent-2411-045: On the contrary, Fan et al. propose D-DPCC~\cite{ijcai2022p126 with an end-to-end learning-based motion estimation and motion compensation (ME/MC) module.}
elements.sent-2411-046: Xia et al.~\cite{xia2023learning further propose a two-layer ME/MC architecture for coarse-to-fine motion compensation.}
elements.sent-2411-047: However, the above ME/MC-based methods are developed only for point cloud video geometry.
elements.sent-2411-048: In addition, their lack of a hierarchical inter- and intra-prediction structure leads to inferior coding efficiency compared to Unicorn-Geometry.
elements.sent-2411-049: Prior to learning-based point cloud compression, there have been significant advances in learned video compression~\cite{lu2019dvc,hu2021fvc,hu2022coarse,li2021deep,sheng2022temporal,li2022hybrid,li2023neural,li2024neural .}
elements.sent-2411-050: DVC~\cite{lu2019dvc and FVC~\cite{hu2021fvc are the pioneering AI video codecs that performs ME/MC on pixel domain and feature domain, respectively.}}
elements.sent-2411-051: These work follow a residual-coding framework which encodes the difference between the warped decoded reference frame and the current frame.
elements.sent-2411-052: Li et al. propose DCVC~\cite{li2021deep , which extends the residual-coding framework to context-coding, generating temporal context priors instead of warped predictions for ME/MC.}
elements.sent-2411-053: DCVC-HEM~\cite{li2022hybrid further proposes a parallel checkerboard entropy model supporting variable bit-rate.}
elements.sent-2411-054: DCVC-DC~\cite{li2023neural and DCVC-FM~\cite{li2024neural further incorporate hierarchical quality structure and inter feature modulation to alleviate the accumulation of error in the temporal context.}}
elements.sent-2411-055: However, due to the irregular point geometry and data diversity of point cloud sequences, learning-based video compression methods cannot be directly integrated to point cloud video compression.
elements.sent-2411-056: As shown in Figure \ref{fig:overall , our method is a hierarchical architecture consisting of $(H-L+1)$ U-Inter and Spatial Predictive Coding Layers, where the $l$-th layer corresponds to the $l$-th level of the point cloud when represented by an octree.}
elements.sent-2411-057: We denote the point cloud frame (including both geometry coordinate and color attribute for each point at each octree level) at time $t$ and layer $l$ as $p_l^{t}$, and its reference ({\it i.e. the decoded previous point cloud frame) as $\hat{p _l^{t-1}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-058: The encoder fuses $p_l^{t}$ and the upper layer's latent $f_{l+1 ^{t}$ to generate the latent representation $f_l^{t}$.}
elements.sent-2411-059: The same encoder is also used to generate the reference frame's latent $\hat{f _l^{t-1}$.}
elements.sent-2411-060: The U-Net structured U-Inter module applies both top-down and bottom-up inter prediction strategies.
elements.sent-2411-061: The top-down motion feature extraction infers the initial motion embedding $e_{d,l ^{t}$ between $f_l^t$ and $f_l^{t-1}$, by exploiting both $f_l^t$ and $\hat{f _l^{t-1}$, as well as the higher-scale motion information $e_{d,l+1 ^{t}$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-062: The bottom-up inter prediction further contextually detach $e_{u,l-1 ^{t}$ from $e_{d,l ^{t}$ to generate motion embedding $e_{l ^{t}$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-063: Essentially, it removes the redundancy of $e_{d,l ^{t}$ conditioned on $e_{u,l-1 ^{t}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-064: The motion compression module compresses and decompresses $e_{l ^{t}$ into $\hat{e _{l ^{t}$, which is then contextually restored (Section \ref{chapter:msintra ) into $e_{u,l ^{t}$ with $e_{u,l-1 ^{t}$ as context.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-065: The motion decoding module decodes the motion field $m_l^{t}$ from $e_{u,l ^{t}$ and the lower layer motion $m_{l-1 ^{t}$ .}}
elements.sent-2411-066: Finally, the network performs motion compensation to generate inter context $\check{f _l^{t}$ with $m_l^t$ by warping and interpolation.}
elements.sent-2411-067: The Spatial Predictive Coding module further reduces the layer-wise spatial redundancy by generating an intra context $\bar{f _l^{t}$ based on the already decoded lower layer's latent $\hat{f _{l-1 ^{t}$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-068: The inter context $\check{f _l^{t}$ and intra context $\bar{f _l^{t}$ are then contextually detached from $f_l^{t}$ to generate residual $r_{l ^{t}$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-069: The latent compression module compresses and decompresses $r_l^{t}$ into $\hat{r _l^{t}$, which is then combined with the context $\bar{f _{l ^{t}$ and $\check{f _l^{t}$ to contextually restore the decoded latent $\hat{f _{l ^{t}$.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-070: The reconstruction module takes $\hat{f _{l ^{t}$ and the previous layer's latent $\hat{p _{l-1 ^{t}$ to recover the color attributes in $\hat{p _l^{t}$.}}}}}

elements.sent-2411-278: Point cloud video (PCV) is a versatile 3D representation of dynamic scenes with emerging applications.
elements.sent-2411-279: We propose a U-Structured inter-frame prediction framework, U-Inter, which performs explicit motion estimation and compensation (ME/MC) at different scales with varying levels of detail.
elements.sent-2411-280: It integrates Top-Down (Fine-to-Coarse) Motion Propagation, Bottom-Up Motion Predictive Coding and Multi-scale Group Motion Compensation to enable accurate motion estimation and efficient motion compression at each scale.
elements.sent-2411-281: In addition, we design a multi-scale spatial-temporal predictive coding module to capture the cross-scale spatial redundancy remaining after U-Inter prediction.
elements.sent-2411-282: We conduct experiments following the MPEG Common Test Condition for dense dynamic point clouds and demonstrate that U-Motion can achieve significant gains over MPEG G-PCC-GesTM v3.0 and recently published learning-based methods for both geometry and attribute compression.
elements.sent-2411-283: One essential element in point cloud video compression is inter-frame prediction, which exploits the temporal redundancy between consecutive frames.
elements.sent-2411-284: However, compared to 2D videos on a regular 2D grid, the points in a PCV are irregularly spread over a small fraction of the 3D space, making the design of motion estimation and compensation approaches for PCV significantly more challenging.
elements.sent-2411-285: The U-Inter structure, akin to the popular U-Net, through the use of both top-down motion propagation, bottom-up motion predictive coding and skip connections, leverages the motion information at both upper and lower scales to inform motion estimation and coding at a current scale.
elements.sent-2411-286: U-Inter also incorporates a multi-scale group motion compensation scheme, utilizing the hierarchically reconstructed motion flow to enrich temporal context generation.
elements.sent-2411-287: We evaluate our method following the MPEG Common Test Conditions (CTC), dense dynamic part's specification~\cite{m66563 for data splitting, in both geometry and color coding.}
elements.sent-2411-288: We conduct ablation studies to validate the effectiveness of each component in our proposed U-Motion codec.
elements.sent-2411-289: We propose a novel unified PCV compression scheme, named U-Motion, which incorporates a hierarchical {\bf U-Inter Prediction module using a U-Net like structure.}
elements.sent-2411-290: The proposed U-Inter incorporates {\bf Top-Down Motion Propagation , {\bf Bottom-Up Motion Predictive Coding and {\bf Multi-scale Group Motion Compensation for accurate motion generation and efficient motion coding under rate constraint.}}}
elements.sent-2411-291: We evaluate the U-Motion codec following the MPEG CTC's data splitting, for both geometry coding and color coding, and demonstrate U-Motion's significant performance gain against both the state-of-the-art learning-based method Unicorn \cite{wang2024versatile2 and MPEG standard G-PCC-GesTM v3.0 \cite{w23041 , in terms of rate-distortion trade-off.}}

elements.sent-2411-387: We present U-Motion, a PCV codec employing both U-structured temporal prediction and coarse-to-fine spatial prediction.
elements.sent-2411-388: The key innovation in U-Motion is its U-inter module, combining multi-scale motion estimation, fine-to-coarse motion propagation and coarse-to-fine predictive coding of motion, to enable effective multi-scale motion compensation under rate constraints.
elements.sent-2411-389: We show U-Motion's applicability to geometry and attribute, achieving significant gains over state-of-the-art methods.
elements.sent-2411-390: Our future work includes multi-frame training to alleviate error-propagation and reducing K-NN complexity for motion compensation.
